chunk_summary: Meal planning for pediatric Type 1 diabetes focuses on matching carbohydrate intake to insulin regimens while supporting growth, activity, and glycemic control. Emphasis is placed on consistent meal structure, carbohydrate estimation, and India-specific cultural considerations.
learning_objectives: Understand the principles of meal planning tailored to insulin regimens in pediatric Type 1 diabetes.
Learn strategies for carbohydrate estimation and insulin-to-carbohydrate ratio adjustments.
Recognize the importance of glycemic index, food combinations, and cultural factors in meal planning.
Identify safety measures for hypoglycemia treatment and sick day management.
key_facts: Restrictive low-carb diets are not recommended for children due to risks of impaired growth and energy intake.
Carbohydrate quality influences postprandial glucose; high-fiber staples, protein, and healthy fats are encouraged.
For fixed-dose insulin regimens, consistent carbohydrate amounts at meals are safer than aggressive variation.
During illness, basal insulin should not be omitted; smaller, frequent carbohydrate portions are recommended.
India-specific considerations include school tiffins, festivals, and cultural dietary practices.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Meal planning in pediatric Type 1 diabetes should support normal growth and activity while matching carbohydrate exposure to the child’s insulin regimen. (ISPAD Clinical Practice Consensus Guidelines 2022)
document_key_topics: Meal planning for pediatric Type 1 diabetes
Safety measures for hypoglycemia and sick days
